Class WorkBatch.WorkItem

java.lang.Object
com.github.mrgarbagegamer.WorkBatch.WorkItem
Enclosing class:
WorkBatch
public static class WorkBatch.WorkItem extends Object
A compact, reusable representation of a range of combinations that share a common prefix.

This is the fundamental unit of work within a WorkBatch. Instead of storing millions of complete combinations, a WorkItem describes a set of combinations by storing a shared prefix and a reference to an array of possible finalClicks. The monkey can then iterate through the final clicks efficiently.

Object Lifecycle

WorkItem instances are pre-allocated within a WorkBatch and are reused to eliminate GC pressure. A generator calls set(short[], int, short[], int) to populate a recycled item, and clear() is called when the WorkBatch itself is recycled.

Since:
2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
See Also:
  • WorkBatch.addWork(short[], int, boolean, int)
Performance Characteristics:
Accessors are O(1). No performance-critical methods.
Threading Model:
Not thread-safe. Instances are owned and operated on by a single thread at a time.
Memory Management:
The object contains references to a prefix array and a final clicks array but does not own the latter. Minimal, fixed overhead per instance.

    Modifier and Type

    private short[]

    The actual length of the content in the prefix array.

  • Field Details

    • prefix

      private short[] prefix
      The shared prefix of the combinations, with a fixed length of prefixLength. The contents of this array are copied from the input provided by set(short[], int, short[], int), ensuring that external modifications do not affect this work item.

      For better performance, this field could be made final and initialized in the constructor.

      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      See Also:
      • getPrefix()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(prefixLength) iteration, O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 2 * (numClicks - 1) bytes for the short[] array.

    • prefixLength

      private int prefixLength
      The actual length of the content in the prefix array. Since the array is always of size numClicks - 1, this field could be removed to save memory.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      See Also:
      • WorkBatch.numClicks
      • getPrefixLength()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes for the primitive int.

    • finalClicks

      private short[] finalClicks
      A reference to either WorkBatch.ODD_CLICK_INDICES or WorkBatch.EVEN_CLICK_INDICES, representing the set of valid final clicks for this work item. The monkey uses this array to complete the combinations, starting from the start index.

      This field does not own the array; it merely holds a reference to one of the static arrays in WorkBatch. It may be worth considering turning this into an enum or a boolean flag to reduce memory usage, though there could be concerns regarding memory locality.

      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      See Also:
      • getFinalClicks()
      • WorkBatch.setClickIndexArrays(short[], short[])
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes as a reference.

    • start

      private int start
      The starting index within finalClicks from which the monkey should begin testing. This allows a generator to create a WorkItem that represents a sub-range of the final clicks.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      See Also:
      • getStart()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes for the primitive int.

  • Constructor Details

    • WorkItem

      WorkItem()
      Constructs a new WorkItem, pre-allocating its internal prefix array based on the static WorkBatch.numClicks value.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if numClicks has not been set.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(numClicks - 1) allocation for the prefix array.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates memory for the prefix array.

  • Method Details

    • set

      void set(short[] prefix, int prefixLength, short[] finalClicks, int start)
      Initializes or re-initializes the WorkItem with its data.

      This method is internally called by WorkBatch.addWork(short[], int, boolean, int) to fill a recycled WorkItem with the necessary data. The provided prefix is copied into the internal array to prevent external modifications from affecting this item.

      Parameters:
      prefix - The common prefix for this range of combinations.
      prefixLength - The length of the prefix.
      finalClicks - The array of possible final clicks (either WorkBatch.ODD_CLICK_INDICES or WorkBatch.EVEN_CLICK_INDICES).
      start - The starting index in the finalClicks array.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(prefixLength) for array copy; O(1) for field assignments.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate; reuses internal arrays.

    • clear

      void clear()
      Resets the WorkItem to a clean state, ready for reuse.

      This is called when the parent WorkBatch is cleared. It clears references to external arrays but does not null out the internal prefix array, allowing it to be recycled without a new allocation.

      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for field assignments.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • getPrefix

      public short[] getPrefix()
      Returns the shared combination prefix for this work item.
      Returns:
      The prefix array.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate; returns reference to existing array.

    • getPrefixLength

      public int getPrefixLength()
      Returns the length of the shared combination prefix.
      Returns:
      The prefix length, or -1 if not set.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • getFinalClicks

      public short[] getFinalClicks()
      Returns the array of possible final clicks for this work range. This will be a reference to either WorkBatch.ODD_CLICK_INDICES or WorkBatch.EVEN_CLICK_INDICES.
      Returns:
      The array of final clicks, or null if not set.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate; returns reference to existing array.

    • getStart

      public int getStart()
      Returns the starting index within the final clicks array from which a monkey should begin processing.
      Returns:
      The start index.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a String representation of the WorkItem, useful for debugging.

      The format is WorkItem{prefix=[...], prefixLength=..., finalClicks=[...]}. The finalClicks portion only includes the elements from the start index to the end of the array.

      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      A String representation of the object.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(prefixLength + finalClicks.length).
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a new StringBuilder and a new String, with indirect allocations from Arrays.copyOfRange(short[], int, int) and Arrays.toString(short[]).

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object obj)
      Compares this WorkItem to another object for equality.

      Two WorkItems are considered equal if their prefix, finalClicks, and start fields are all equal. The prefixLength is not compared as it is derived from the prefix array.

      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      Parameters:
      obj - The Object to compare with.
      Returns:
      true if the objects are equal, false otherwise.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(prefixLength + finalClicks.length) in the worst case due to array comparisons.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • hashCode

      public int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for the object.

      The hash code is calculated based on the contents of the prefix and finalClicks arrays, as well as the start index.

      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object
      Returns:
      A hash code value for this object.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(prefixLength + finalClicks.length) due to array hash code computations.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

Performance-optimized brute-force solver for hexagonal grid Lights Out puzzles
